title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 7 (Fragment 4)
text:
Then somebody cast yet another spell.

This time, it was a thick wet fog. No doubt, at the beginning, it would not have seemed so terrible, but, after all the rest, just when the fight was over, Paolo felt it was the last straw. All he could see, in front of his eyes, was thick whiteness. After he had taken a breath or so, he was coughing. He could hear coughing all around, and far off into the distance, which was the only thing which showed him he was not entirely alone. He turned his head from trying to see who else was coughing, and found he could not see Lucia. Nor could he find Bernardo, and he knew he had been holding Bernardo’s arm a second before. As soon as he realized that, he found he had lost his sense of direction too. He was all alone, coughing and shivering, in cold white emptiness.

“I am not going to lose my head,” Paolo told himself sternly. “My father didn’t, and so I shan’t. I shall find somewhere to shelter until this beastly spell is over. Then I shall go home. I don’t care if Tonino is still missing—” He stopped then, because a thought came to him, like an astonishing discovery. “We’re never going to find Tonino this way, anyway,” he said. And he knew it was true.

With his hands stretched out in front of him and his eyes spread very wide in hopes of seeing something—which was unlikely, since they were streaming from the fog, and so was his nose—Paolo coughed and sniffed and shuffled his way forward until his toes came up against stone. Paolo looked down, but he could not see what it was. He tried lifting one foot, with his toes scraping against the obstruction. And, after a few inches, the obstruction stopped and his foot shot forward. It was a ledge, then. Probably the curb. He had been near the edge of the road when he ran away from the elephant. He got both feet on the curb and shuffled forward six inches—then he fell upstairs over what seemed to be a body.

It gave Paolo such a shock that he dared not move at first. But he soon realized that the body beneath him was shivering, as he was, and trying to cough and mutter at the same time. “Holy Mary—” Paolo heard, in a hoarse blurred voice. Very puzzled, Paolo put out a careful hand and felt the body. His fingers met cold metal buttons, uniform braid, and, a little above that, a warm face—which gave a croak as Paolo’s cold hand met its mouth—and a large furry moustache beneath the nose.

Angel of Caprona! Paolo thought. It’s the Chief of Police!

Paolo got himself to his knees on what must be the steps of the Art Gallery. There was no one around he could ask, but it did not seem fair to leave someone lying helpless in the fog. It was bad enough if you could move. So hoping he was doing the right thing, Paolo knelt and sang, very softly, the most general cancel-spell he could think of. It had no effect on the fog—that was evidently very strong magic—but he heard the Chief of Police roll over on his side and groan. Boots scraped as he tested his legs. “Mamma mia!” Paolo heard him moan.

He sounded as if he wanted to be alone. Paolo left him and crawled his way up the Gallery steps. He had no idea he had reached the top, until he hit his elbow on a pillar and drove his head into Lucia’s stomach at the same moment. Both of them said some extremely unpleasant things.

vocabulary.definition: very surprising; difficult to believe
vocabulary.example: I don’t care if Tonino is still missing—” He stopped then, because a thought came to him, like an astonishing discovery.

vocabulary.definition: interwoven strands of hair or thread, or decorative trims on clothing
vocabulary.example: His fingers met cold metal buttons, uniform braid, and, a little above that, a warm face—which gave a croak as Paolo’s cold hand met its mouth—and a large furry moustache beneath the nose.

vocabulary.definition: a magical incantation used to nullify or undo another spell
vocabulary.example: So hoping he was doing the right thing, Paolo knelt and sang, very softly, the most general cancel-spell he could think of.

vocabulary.definition: to throw something forcefully
vocabulary.example: Then somebody cast yet another spell.

vocabulary.definition: to expel air from the lungs with a sudden sharp sound
vocabulary.example: After he had taken a breath or so, he was coughing.

vocabulary.definition: an edge between a sidewalk and a street
vocabulary.example: Probably the curb.

vocabulary.definition: the line or course along which something moves or points
vocabulary.example: As soon as he realized that, he found he had lost his sense of direction too.

vocabulary.definition: the act or process of finding something unknown
vocabulary.example: I don’t care if Tonino is still missing—” He stopped then, because a thought came to him, like an astonishing discovery.

vocabulary.definition: the amount of space between two points
vocabulary.example: He could hear coughing all around, and far off into the distance, which was the only thing which showed him he was not entirely alone.

vocabulary.definition: a change that is a result or consequence of an action
vocabulary.example: It had no effect on the fog—that was evidently very strong magic—but he heard the Chief of Police roll over on his side and groan.

vocabulary.definition: to make a deep sound in response to pain or discomfort
vocabulary.example: It had no effect on the fog—that was evidently very strong magic—but he heard the Chief of Police roll over on his side and groan.

vocabulary.definition: unable to defend oneself or act effectively
vocabulary.example: There was no one around he could ask, but it did not seem fair to leave someone lying helpless in the fog.

vocabulary.definition: having a rough or harsh voice
vocabulary.example: “Holy Mary—” Paolo heard, in a hoarse blurred voice.

vocabulary.definition: a narrow horizontal surface projecting from a wall or cliff
vocabulary.example: It was a ledge, then.

vocabulary.definition: to complain or express pain in a low sound
vocabulary.example: ” Paolo heard him moan.

vocabulary.definition: hair grown on the upper lip
vocabulary.example: His fingers met cold metal buttons, uniform braid, and, a little above that, a warm face—which gave a croak as Paolo’s cold hand met its mouth—and a large furry moustache beneath the nose.

vocabulary.definition: to speak quietly and indistinctly
vocabulary.example: But he soon realized that the body beneath him was shivering, as he was, and trying to cough and mutter at the same time.

vocabulary.definition: something that blocks or impedes progress or movement
vocabulary.example: He tried lifting one foot, with his toes scraping against the obstruction.

vocabulary.definition: a vertical structure used as support or decoration
vocabulary.example: He had no idea he had reached the top, until he hit his elbow on a pillar and drove his head into Lucia’s stomach at the same moment.

vocabulary.definition: a place giving protection from bad weather or danger
vocabulary.example: I shall find somewhere to shelter until this beastly spell is over.

vocabulary.definition: to shake slightly because of cold or fear
vocabulary.example: He was all alone, coughing and shivering, in cold white emptiness.

vocabulary.definition: a sudden upsetting or surprising event or experience
vocabulary.example: It gave Paolo such a shock that he dared not move at first.

vocabulary.definition: a form of words used as a magical charm or incantation
vocabulary.example: Then somebody cast yet another spell.

vocabulary.definition: in a serious and unrelenting manner
vocabulary.example: “I am not going to lose my head,” Paolo told himself sternly.

vocabulary.definition: very bad or unpleasant
vocabulary.example: No doubt, at the beginning, it would not have seemed so terrible, but, after all the rest, just when the fight was over, Paolo felt it was the last straw.

vocabulary.definition: special clothing worn by members of an organization
vocabulary.example: His fingers met cold metal buttons, uniform braid, and, a little above that, a warm face—which gave a croak as Paolo’s cold hand met its mouth—and a large furry moustache beneath the nose.

vocabulary.definition: causing discomfort or unhappiness
vocabulary.example: Both of them said some extremely unpleasant things.

vocabulary.definition: the quality or state of being white or pale
vocabulary.example: All he could see, in front of his eyes, was thick whiteness.
